title: Koduthathellaam Koduthaan Song Lyrics Padagotti
stanzas:
Koduthathellaam koduthaan
avan yaarukkaaga koduthaan
orutharukkaa koduthaan?
illai, oorukkaaga koduthaan.(2)

Mankudisai vaasal endraal
thendral vara veruthidumaa(2)
maalai nilaa yaezhai endraal
velicham thara maruthidumaa

Koduthathellaam koduthaan
avan yaarukkaaga koduthaan
orutharukkaa koduthaan?
illai, oorukkaaga koduthaan

padaithavan mael
pazhiyum illai
pasithavan mel
paavam illai.
kidaitharvagal pirithukondaar
uzhaithavargal theruvil nindraar

palar vaada vaada
silar vazha vazha
orupodhum dheivam
koduthathillai.

Koduthathellaam koduthaan
avan yaarukkaaga koduthaan
orutharukkaa koduthaan?
illai, oorukkaaga koduthaan

madi niraya porul irukkum
manam neraya irul irukkum
edhuvantha podhum
podhuvendru vaithu
vazhkindra perai
vazhthiduvom

Koduthathellaam koduthaan
avan yaarukkaaga koduthaan
orutharukkaa koduthaan?
illai, oorukkaaga koduthaan

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Koduthathellaam Koduthaan?

The song emphasizes selflessness and the importance of contributing to the community.

What is the context of the song in the movie Padagotti?

The song reflects the protagonist's dedication to the welfare of his people.

Who composed the music for Koduthathellaam Koduthaan?

The music was composed by the legendary duo Viswanathan–Ramamoorthy.

When was the song Koduthathellaam Koduthaan released?

The song was released in 1970.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Koduthathellaam Koduthaan is a classic Tamil song from the movie Padagotti, released in 1970. The song beautifully conveys themes of selflessness and community service, reflecting the protagonist's dedication to his people. With its motivational and nostalgic tone, it evokes a sense of pride and unity. The music, composed by Viswanathan–Ramamoorthy, complements the powerful lyrics, making it a memorable piece in Tamil cinema history.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Koduthathellaam Koduthaan from the classic film Padagotti is a timeless melody that captures the essence of selflessness and community spirit. With its evocative lyrics and soothing composition, this song remains a favorite among fans of Tamil cinema. Dive into the nostalgic vibes and experience the magic of MGR's era through this unforgettable tune.
stanzaMeanings: He gave everything he had. For whom did he give it? Did he give it for one person? No, he gave it for the whole town.
Did he give it for one person? No, he gave it for the whole town.
If the wind comes to the entrance of a poor hut, will it refuse to enter? If the moonlight sees a poor person, will it refuse to give light?
For you, for me, God did not give anything separately.
He gave everything he had. For whom did he give it? Did he give it for one person? No, he gave it for the whole town.
There is no blame on the creator, and no sin on the hungry. Those who got things separated, and those who worked stood on the streets.
Some people suffer, some people thrive, but God never gave anything equally.
He gave everything he had. For whom did he give it? Did he give it for one person? No, he gave it for the whole town.
While there are people who say 'no,' there are others who say 'yes.'
There are many things in the lap, but the heart is full of darkness. Let's praise those who live with whatever they have.
He gave everything he had. For whom did he give it? Did he give it for one person? No, he gave it for the whole town.
